Title: The Acer truncatum genome provides insights into nervonic acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / nervonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acid elongation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Rate-limiting condensation step of ER elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C24:1 nervonic acid after three elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation reaction in each elongation cycle, extending oleyl-CoA-derived acyl-CoA by two carbons per cycle in the ER pathway leading to nervonic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of the very-long-chain monounsaturated product through repeated elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil; cotyledon/endosperm oil bodies of developing and mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported as helpful in treatment of brain diseases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in mature seeds; significantly upregulated from 85 to 100 DAF during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in mature seeds than root, stem, leaf, and flower; upregulated 4.74-fold between 85 and 100 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate key enzyme promoting nervonic acid biosynthesis in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome analysis; seed-stage metabolite profiling by GC-MS; qRT-PCR across six seed stages and tissues; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a candidate rate-limiting/key control enzyme because KCS is stated to be rate-limiting and this gene is specifically induced at the onset of nervonic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA557096; BioSample SAMN12389479; SRA study SUB6287730); RNA-seq of five tissues and six seed stages; metabolomics/fatty acid profiling by GC-MS; WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or metabolic engineering to increase seed nervonic acid; should be prioritized for functional validation and possibly stacked with complementary elongation-pathway regulators
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chr4.2307.KCS is one of three KCS genes whose expression pattern matches nervonic acid accumulation during seed development. 2. It is significantly upregulated at the critical transition from 85 to 100 DAF when nervonic acid first appears. 3. It is more highly expressed in mature seeds than in other tissues, suggesting a central role in seed nervonic acid production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of 10 tandemly duplicated KCS genes clustered in a 0.27-Mb region on pseudochromosome 4; identified as part of a small phylogenetic branch associated with candidate VLCFA/nervonic acid synthesis genes.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / nervonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acid elongation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Rate-limiting condensation step of ER elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C24:1 nervonic acid after three elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation reaction in each elongation cycle, extending oleyl-CoA-derived acyl-CoA by two carbons per cycle in the ER pathway leading to nervonic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of the very-long-chain monounsaturated product through repeated elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil; cotyledon/endosperm oil bodies of developing and mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported as helpful in treatment of brain diseases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in mature seeds; significantly upregulated from 85 to 100 DAF during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in mature seeds than root, stem, leaf, and flower; upregulated 4.25-fold between 85 and 100 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate key enzyme promoting nervonic acid biosynthesis in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome analysis; seed-stage metabolite profiling by GC-MS; qRT-PCR across six seed stages and tissues; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a candidate rate-limiting/key control enzyme because KCS is stated to be rate-limiting and this gene is specifically induced at the onset of nervonic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA557096; BioSample SAMN12389479; SRA study SUB6287730); RNA-seq of five tissues and six seed stages; metabolomics/fatty acid profiling by GC-MS; WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority candidate for engineering increased nervonic acid content in A. truncatum seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chr4.2308.KCS is one of three KCS genes most strongly associated with nervonic acid accumulation. 2. Its expression increases sharply at the onset of nervonic acid production between 85 and 100 DAF. 3. It is strongly seed-enriched relative to vegetative tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tandemly duplicated within the chromosome 4 KCS cluster and grouped in the same small phylogenetic branch as the other candidate nervonic acid KCS genes.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / nervonic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acid elongation branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Rate-limiting condensation step of ER elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C24:1 nervonic acid after three elongation cycles
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensation reaction in each elongation cycle, extending oleyl-CoA-derived acyl-CoA by two carbons per cycle in the ER pathway leading to nervonic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of the very-long-chain monounsaturated product through repeated elongation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed oil; cotyledon/endosperm oil bodies of developing and mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported as helpful in treatment of brain diseases
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in mature seeds; significantly upregulated from 85 to 100 DAF during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in mature seeds than root, stem, leaf, and flower; upregulated 3.28-fold between 85 and 100 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate key enzyme promoting nervonic acid biosynthesis in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptome analysis; seed-stage metabolite profiling by GC-MS; qRT-PCR across six seed stages and tissues; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a candidate rate-limiting/key control enzyme because KCS is stated to be rate-limiting and this gene is specifically induced at the onset of nervonic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA557096; BioSample SAMN12389479; SRA study SUB6287730); RNA-seq of five tissues and six seed stages; metabolomics/fatty acid profiling by GC-MS; WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate gene for enhancing nervonic acid accumulation in seeds, potentially in combination with co-regulatory TFs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Chr4.2311.KCS shows a seed-development expression pattern concordant with nervonic acid accumulation. 2. It is significantly induced between 85 and 100 DAF, when nervonic acid becomes detectable. 3. It is highly expressed in mature seeds compared with other tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Member of the tandemly duplicated chromosome 4 KCS cluster; highlighted alongside Chr4.2307.KCS and Chr4.2308.KCS as the three most likely genes contributing to nervonic acid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported as helpful in treatment of brain diseases
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Very-long-chain fatty acid / nervonic acid biosynthesis regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Candidate NA biosynthesis genes including KCS genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as candidate regulators associated with nervonic acid biosynthesis during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA based on RNA-seq across seed developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated candidate regulator, not functionally validated as decisive
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in black co-expression module positively related to nervonic acid biosynthesis during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of seed developmental stages; WGCNA; genome annotation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream targets to coordinate increased nervonic acid synthesis, but require direct functional validation before deployment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB transcription factors were enriched among genes in the WGCNA black module positively associated with nervonic acid biosynthesis. 2. Prior literature cited in the paper supports MYB roles in fatty acid and VLCFA regulation. 3. In this study they remain candidate regulators without direct target validation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific MYB gene IDs were not provided in the main text; only family-level implication from Table S26/WGCNA.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported as helpful in treatment of brain diseases
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Very-long-chain fatty acid / nervonic acid biosynthesis regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Candidate NA biosynthesis genes including KCS genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as candidate regulators associated with nervonic acid biosynthesis during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA based on RNA-seq across seed developmental stages
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-associated candidate regulator, not functionally validated as decisive
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in black co-expression module positively related to nervonic acid biosynthesis during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of seed developmental stages; WGCNA; genome annotation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-regulators for trait engineering, but direct mechanistic validation is needed
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP transcription factors were found in the WGCNA module positively related to nervonic acid biosynthesis. 2. The paper cites previous evidence that bZIP factors regulate fatty acid synthesis. 3. Their role here is candidate-level and not directly validated.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific bZIP gene IDs were not provided in the main text; implication is based on module membership reported in Table S26.
